Try DevTools → Network → Fast/Slow 3G. Wire savings matter more under latency.

capnwasm playground

Live in-browser bench. Fetch 200 user records in parallel from static fixtures, decode, render. Same data both sides — only the wire format and decoder differ.

← Landing· Playground· Render bench· Snippets· Dynamic· OpenAPI· Wire inspector· Chat· Notes· vs capnweb

Not production-ready yet. Normal readers now keep message bytes in managed WebAssembly.Memory, but this remains a controlled benchmark surface while allocator, large-data, hostile-input, and secure-memory hardening continues.

REST / JSON fetch + JSON.parse
fetch—
decode—
render—
total—
wire bytes—
egress @ 1k r/s—
    capnweb fetch + capnweb.deserialize

    decode—

    total—
    wire bytes—
    egress @ 1k r/s—
      capnwasm fetch + Cap'n Proto reader

      decode—

        General workload suite (auto-runs after RPC)

        Same browser, same wasm runtime, no network. These rows cover the common shapes the fetch demo does not: sparse metadata, numeric view(), dynamic schemas, and list scans.

        Waiting for RPC bench…
